html:
[2830]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 受危险于虎争之际 - Facing danger during times of fierce competition.
  • 胼胝戈甲寝寤风霜 - Enduring hardship and exposure to the elements, calloused hands from hard work, weapons and armor, sleep and waking, cold winds and frost.
  • 申武力而拘诸原 - To demonstrate military strength and capture the enemy in the plains.
  • 立战功而平多垒 - To establish military achievements and level many fortresses.
  • 亡躯致命 - To lay down one's life and dedicate oneself completely.
  • 事主勤王 - To serve the ruler and be diligent in the state's affairs.
  • 鸿基覇盛大业 - The great foundation and mighty empire.
  • 雄崇地及雍凉界连南北德通吴越威定蛮陬 - A mighty and elevated territory, boundaries stretching from north to south, virtue spreading to Wu and Yue, establishment of authority over remote regions.
  • 郡府颇多关河渐广 - Many prefectures and cities, with borders gradually expanding.
  • 人物秀丽土地繁华 - People are refined and the land is prosperous.
  • 四海辐裂之秋 - The autumn of the world's fragmentation.
  • 万代龙兴之业 - The eternal legacy of the dragon's rise.
  • 陛下生居富贵坐得乾坤 - Your Majesty, born into wealth and power, sits in control of the world.
  • 好欢娱不思机变 - Enjoying pleasure without thinking of strategy.
  • 以名教而自节以礼乐而自防 - To regulate oneself through the teachings of Confucianism and to guard oneself through ritual and music.
  • 循道德之规受师傅之训 - To follow the rules of morality and to receive the teachings of mentors.
  • 知社稷之不易想稼穑之最难 - To understand the difficulty of maintaining the state and to think of the hard work of farming.
  • 惜高祖之基扃似太宗之临御 - To cherish the foundation laid by the High Ancestor and to emulate the governance of the Great Ancestor.
  • 贤贤易色孜孜为心 - To value virtue over appearance and to be diligent in one's heart.
  • 无稽之言勿听弗询之谋勿用 - Do not listen to baseless words and do not use unconsulted plans.
  • 少止息于诸处林亭 - To rest less frequently in various places.
  • 多观览于前王经史 - To frequently observe and study the histories and classics of past kings.
  • 别修上德用卜远图 - To cultivate superior virtue and to plan for distant goals.
  • 莫遣色荒 - Do not indulge in sensual pleasures.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 虎争 - Symbolizes intense competition or conflict, often used to describe the struggles for power.
  • 高祖 - Refers to the founder of the dynasty, typically the first emperor or ruler.
  • 太宗 - Refers to a revered ancestor, often the second emperor or a significant ruler.
  • 五音 - Refers to the five tones of music, which are fundamental in Chinese music theory.
  • 三镜 - Refers to the three mirrors of a ruler, which are used to reflect on one's actions and governance.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • The long sentence '歴艰辛于草昜之中受危险于虎争之际' describes the hardships and dangers faced during the early and competitive stages of the empire's development.
  • The complex sentence '胼胝戈甲寝寤风霜申武力而拘诸原立战功而平多垒' describes the physical and military efforts made to establish and expand the empire.
  • The sentence '亡躯致命事主勤王方得成家至于开国今日鸿基覇盛大业' outlines the sacrifices and efforts made to establish the empire and achieve its grandeur.
  • The sentence '雄崇地及雍凉界连南北德通吴越威定蛮陬郡府颇多关河渐广人物秀丽土地繁华' describes the extent and prosperity of the empire, including its territories, governance, and cultural richness.
  • The sentence '四海辐裂之秋万代龙兴之业' describes the fragmentation of the world and the rise of a powerful dynasty.
  • The sentence '陛下生居富贵坐得乾坤好欢娱不思机变' describes the emperor's position of power and the potential dangers of complacency.
  • The sentence '以名教而自节以礼乐而自防循道德之规受师傅之训知社稷之不易想稼穑之最难' outlines the emperor's responsibilities and the importance of moral and practical governance.
  • The sentence '惜高祖之基扃似太宗之临御贤贤易色孜孜为心无稽之言勿听弗询之谋勿用' emphasizes the importance of valuing virtue and avoiding baseless actions.
  • The sentence '听五音而受谏以三镜而照怀少止息于诸处林亭多观览于前王经史别修上德用卜远图莫遣色荒' describes the emperor's duties in listening to advice, reflecting on his actions, and planning for the future.

Proper Nouns

  • 高祖 - The founder of the dynasty.
  • 太宗 - A revered ancestor, often the second emperor or a significant ruler.
  • 五音 - The five tones of music.
  • 三镜 - The three mirrors of a ruler.

[2831]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 毋令酒惑 - Classical warning meaning 'do not let wine lead you astray.'
  • 常亲政事 - Means 'always be close to governing affairs,' emphasizing the ruler's duty to be personally involved in governance.
  • 勿恣闲游 - Means 'do not indulge in idle wandering,' warning against frivolous or excessive leisure.
  • 臣窃闻 - A humble expression meaning 'I humbly hear' or 'I have heard,' used by a subject addressing a ruler.
  • 巡边垒 - Means 'to tour the border fortresses,' referring to the ruler's inspection of frontier defenses.
  • 天水地远 - A classical phrase meaning 'the land of Tianshui is far,' referring to a distant region.
  • 峻恶难行 - Means 'steep and evil, difficult to traverse,' describing harsh terrain.
  • 险栈欹云 - A metaphorical phrase meaning 'dangerous mountain paths leaning against clouds,' evoking treacherous travel.
  • 危揷汉 - A metaphorical phrase meaning 'high peaks piercing the sky,' emphasizing the height and danger of the mountains.
  • 微雨则吹摧阁道 - Means 'even a light rain can blow down the walkways,' highlighting the fragility of the infrastructure.
  • 稍泥则沮滑山程 - Means 'a little mud can make the mountain journey slippery,' emphasizing the difficulty of travel.
  • 鸣銮 - Means 'to sound the bell of the carriage,' a sign of royal presence.
  • 叱驭 - Means 'to scold the driver,' a metaphor for the difficulty of controlling a situation.
  • 敌京咫尺 - Means 'the enemy is just a step away,' emphasizing the proximity of danger.
  • 塞邑荒凉 - Means 'the frontier town is desolate,' describing the bleakness of the frontier.
  • 民杂蕃戎 - Means 'the people are mixed with barbarians,' referring to the presence of non-Han ethnic groups.
  • 地多岚瘴 - Means 'the land is full of mist and瘴气 (miasma),' emphasizing the unhealthy environment.
  • 华风异景 - Means 'Chinese customs and strange sights,' referring to the cultural and natural attractions of the capital.
  • 选胜寻幽 - Means 'to seek out scenic spots and explore secluded places,' describing leisurely pursuits.
  • 陇水声悲 - Means 'the sound of the Long River is mournful,' evoking a sense of sorrow.
  • 胡笳韵咽 - Means 'the sound of the Huqin (a type of flute) is choked,' emphasizing the melancholic tone.
  • 带甲之士 - Means 'men in armor,' referring to soldiers.
  • 枕戈之人 - Means 'those who sleep with their weapons,' emphasizing the readiness for battle.
  • 探虏于孤峯 - Means 'to scout the enemy on isolated peaks,' describing military surveillance.
  • 朝朝疑虑 - Means 'day by day with suspicion,' emphasizing constant vigilance.
  • 望旗于峻岭 - Means 'to watch the flag on the steep mountains,' describing military observation.
  • 多山足水 - Means 'abundant in mountains and water,' describing the geography.
  • 易动难安 - Means 'easily disturbed but hard to settle,' emphasizing the instability of the region.
  • 麦积崖无可瞻恋 - Means 'the Maiji Gorge offers nothing to admire,' describing the lack of scenic value.
  • 米谷峡何亚连知 - Means 'the Migu Gorge is no better known,' emphasizing the obscurity of the region.
  • 路遇嗟山程 - Means 'meeting on the road laments the mountain journey,' expressing the hardships of travel.
  • 通怨水 - Means 'the water carries complaints,' emphasizing the negative aspects of the environment.
  • 秦穆圉马之地 - Refers to the land of Qin Mu's horse stables, a historical allusion to the region's past.
  • 隗嚣僭位之 - Refers to the usurper Wei Xiu's territory, a historical allusion to the region's past.
  • 一人出行百司参从 - Means 'one person's journey is accompanied by a hundred officials,' emphasizing the scale of the entourage.
  • 千群雾拥 - Means 'thousands of people are surrounded by fog,' emphasizing the difficulty of travel.
  • 万众星驰 - Means 'ten thousand people are like stars racing,' emphasizing the speed and scale of the journey.
  • 当路州县摧残 - Means 'the state and counties along the way are ruined,' emphasizing the impact of the journey.
  • 所在馆驿隘少 - Means 'the inns and stations everywhere are narrow and few,' emphasizing the lack of accommodations.
  • 止宿尚犹不易 - Means 'even stopping for the night is not easy,' emphasizing the difficulty of finding lodging.
  • 供须固是为难 - Means 'providing for the journey is indeed difficult,' emphasizing the logistical challenges.
  • 纵若就中指挥自破 - Means 'even if you command it yourself, it will break apart,' emphasizing the inevitability of failure.
  • 属省钱物未 - Means 'the provincial funds and goods are not,' emphasizing the lack of resources.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 天水 - Refers to the ancient region of Tianshui, known for its harsh terrain and strategic importance.
  • 陇水 - Refers to the Long River, a significant geographical feature in the region.
  • 胡笳 - Refers to the Huqin, a type of flute used in military and ceremonial contexts.
  • 带甲之士 - Refers to soldiers, emphasizing the military presence in the region.
  • 枕戈之人 - Refers to those who sleep with their weapons, emphasizing the readiness for battle.
  • 探虏于孤峯 - Refers to military surveillance, emphasizing the strategic importance of the region.
  • 朝朝疑虑 - Refers to constant vigilance, emphasizing the threat of enemy action.
  • 望旗于峻岭 - Refers to military observation, emphasizing the strategic importance of the region.
  • 多山足水 - Refers to the geography of the region, emphasizing the challenges of travel.
  • 易动难安 - Refers to the instability of the region, emphasizing the difficulty of governance.
  • 麦积崖 - Refers to the Maiji Gorge, a significant geographical feature in the region.
  • 米谷峡 - Refers to the Migu Gorge, a significant geographical feature in the region.
  • 秦穆圉马之地 - Refers to the land of Qin Mu's horse stables, a historical allusion to the region's past.
  • 隗嚣僭位之 - Refers to the usurper Wei Xiu's territory, a historical allusion to the region's past.

Syntactic Ambiguities

  • 毋令酒惑常亲政事勿恣闲游 - This long sentence must be read as a single warning against indulgence in wine and idle wandering.
  • 臣窃闻陛下欲出成都往巡边垒 - This clause introduces the subject's knowledge of the ruler's intention to tour the border fortresses.
  • 且天水地远峻恶难行险栈欹云危揷汉 - This long clause describes the harsh terrain and difficulty of travel.
  • 微雨则吹摧阁道稍泥则沮滑山程 - This clause emphasizes the fragility of the infrastructure and the difficulty of travel.
  • 鸣銮那堪叱驭 - This clause emphasizes the difficulty of controlling the situation.
  • 敌京咫尺塞邑荒凉民杂蕃戎地多岚瘴 - This clause describes the proximity of danger and the bleakness of the frontier.
  • 华风异景不可选胜寻幽 - This clause emphasizes the lack of scenic value and the difficulty of leisurely pursuits.
  • 陇水声悲胡笳韵咽 - This clause evokes a sense of sorrow and melancholy.
  • 带甲之士城上宿枕戈之人 - This clause emphasizes the military presence and readiness for battle.
  • 探虏于孤峯朝朝疑虑 - This clause emphasizes the constant vigilance and threat of enemy action.
  • 望旗于峻岭日日堤防 - This clause emphasizes the constant caution and threat of enemy action.
  • 多山足水之乡即易动难安之地 - This clause emphasizes the instability of the region.
  • 麦积崖无可瞻恋米谷峡何亚连知 - This clause emphasizes the lack of scenic value and the obscurity of the region.
  • 路遇嗟山程通怨水 - This clause emphasizes the hardships of travel and the negative aspects of the environment.
  • 秦穆圉马之地隗嚣僭位之 - This clause refers to historical allusions to the region's past.
  • 一人出行百司参从千群雾拥万众星驰 - This clause emphasizes the scale of the entourage and the difficulty of travel.

[2832]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 免因依扰践触处凌迟 - Refers to the avoidance of unnecessary trouble or disturbance, with '凌迟' (lingchi) possibly metaphorically used to mean severe consequences.
  • 苍龙出海云行雨施 - Metaphorical expression referring to the movement of powerful forces or leaders, with '苍龙' (canglong) meaning 'blue dragon,' a symbol of imperial power or authority.
  • 岂教浪静风恬 - Rhetorical question meaning 'how can one expect calm waters and gentle winds?' suggesting that peace and tranquility are not to be expected in the current situation.
  • 必见伤苗损稼 - Literally 'necessarily see the damage to crops,' implying that any action will inevitably cause harm or disruption.
  • 銮舆须止天歩难移 - '銮舆' (luanyu) refers to the imperial carriage, and '天歩' (tianbu) means 'heavenly steps' or 'divine will.' This phrase suggests that the imperial carriage must stop, and the divine will is difficult to change.
  • 况顷年大驾只到山南犹不闗进发兵士 - '大驾' (dajia) refers to the imperial entourage, and '山南' (shan nan) means 'south of the mountains.' This phrase indicates that even when the imperial entourage went as far as the south of the mountains, it did not involve the mobilization of troops.
  • 复闻大驾重来 - '复闻' (fu wen) means 'to hear again,' and '重来' (chong lai) means 'to come again.' This phrase suggests that there is news of the imperial entourage coming again.
  • 若彼预有计谋此则更须征讨 - '若彼' (ruo bi) means 'if they,' and '预有计谋' (yu you jimu) means 'have a premeditated plan.' This phrase suggests that if the enemy has a premeditated plan, then further military action is necessary.
  • 况鳯翔久为进敌必贮奸谋 - '鳯翔' (fengxiang) is a place name, and '久为进敌' (jiu wei jin di) means 'has long been a place for advancing enemies.' This phrase suggests that Fengxiang has been a place where enemies have long been advancing and must have hidden plans.
  • 切虑妄搆妖词致生衅隙 - '切虑' (qie lv) means 'to deeply consider,' '妄搆' (wang gou) means 'to fabricate,' and '妖词' (yao ci) means 'evil words.' This phrase suggests that one must deeply consider the possibility of fabricated evil words leading to conflicts.
  • 又陛下与唐主始申欢好信币交驰 - '陛下' (bei xia) means 'Your Majesty,' and '唐主' (tang zhu) refers to the ruler of the Tang dynasty. This phrase suggests that the emperor and the Tang ruler have recently renewed friendly relations and exchanged gifts.
  • 但虑闻道圣驾亲行别懐疑忌 - '但虑' (dan lv) means 'but to worry,' '闻道' (wen dao) means 'to hear of,' and '圣驾亲行' (sheng ja qin xing) means 'the imperial entourage personally traveling.' This phrase suggests that one worries about the Tang ruler hearing of the emperor's personal travel and harboring suspicions.
  • 其必特差使命请陛下境上防盟 - '其必' (qi bi) means 'it is certain that,' '特差' (te cha) means 'to specially send,' and '使命' (shi ming) means 'envoys.' This phrase suggests that it is certain that special envoys will be sent to request the emperor to meet on the border for a defensive alliance.
  • 未审圣躬去与不去 - '未审' (wei shen) means 'to not know,' '圣躬' (sheng gong) means 'the emperor's body,' and '去与不去' (qu yu bu qu) means 'to go or not to go.' This phrase suggests that it is not known whether the emperor will go or not.
  • 若去则相似秦赵争强彼此难屈 - '若去' (ruo qu) means 'if he goes,' '相似' (xiang si) means 'similar to,' '秦赵' (qin zhao) refers to the states of Qin and Zhao, and '争强' (zheng qiang) means 'to compete for strength.' This phrase suggests that if the emperor goes, it will be similar to the competition between Qin and Zhao, making it difficult for both sides to yield.
  • 若不去即便同鲁衞不睦战伐寻兴 - '若不去' (ruo bu qu) means 'if he does not go,' '同鲁衞' (tong lu wei) means 'the same as Lu and Wei,' and '不睦' (bu mu) means 'not harmonious.' This phrase suggests that if the emperor does not go, it will be the same as the lack of harmony between Lu and Wei, leading to war.
  • 酌彼未萌料其 - '酌彼' (zhuo bi) means 'to consider that,' '未萌' (wei meng) means 'before it sprouts,' and '料其' (liao qi) means 'to estimate it.' This phrase suggests that one should consider and estimate the situation before it develops.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 苍龙出海云行雨施 - Metaphorical expression, possibly referring to the movement of powerful forces or leaders, with '苍龙' (canglong) meaning 'blue dragon,' a symbol of imperial power or authority.
  • 銮舆须止天歩难移 - '銮舆' (luanyu) refers to the imperial carriage, and '天歩' (tianbu) means 'heavenly steps' or 'divine will.' This phrase suggests that the imperial carriage must stop, and the divine will is difficult to change.
  • 鳯翔 - A place name, historically significant in Chinese history.
  • 秦赵 - Refers to the states of Qin and Zhao, known for their conflicts in ancient Chinese history.
  • 鲁衞 - Refers to the states of Lu and Wei, known for their lack of harmony in ancient Chinese history.
  • 唐主 - Refers to the ruler of the Tang dynasty, a significant period in Chinese history.

[2833]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 先见 - Term of address meaning 'Your Majesty' or 'Your Highness.'
  • 思忖 - To consider or reflect upon.
  • 省方巡狩 - Term referring to the ancient Chinese practice of the emperor traveling to various regions to inspect governance and administer justice.
  • 吊民伐罪 - To console the people and punish the guilty, indicating a mission of justice.
  • 展义观风 - To extend righteousness and observe customs, suggesting the emperor's role in promoting virtue and understanding local conditions.
  • 未闻一件教条 - Not hearing of any specific edict or command.
  • 驱策人马 - Driving and directing troops, referring to military movements.
  • 秦则舟船几溺 - In Qin, the boats nearly sank, a reference to the dangers faced during Qin's expeditions.
  • 青城则嫔防沈 - In Qingcheng, the concubines drowned, another reference to the risks of imperial travel.
  • 自取惊忧 - Causing one's own alarm and worry.
  • 为何切事 - For what urgent matter.
  • 却还京辇 - Returning to the capital.
  • 不悦军民 - Not pleasing the soldiers and civilians.
  • 但郁众情 - Only causing the public's emotions to be suppressed.
  • 莫彰帝德 - Not displaying the emperor's virtue.
  • 忆昔先皇在日 - Recalling the days of the previous emperor.
  • 未尝无故巡游 - Never undertaking tours without a reason.
  • 率意频离宫阙 - Frequent departures from the palace on a whim.
  • 劳心费力 - Wasting mental effort and physical energy.
  • 有何所为 - What is the purpose of these actions.
  • 依前整跸 - Preparing for another journey.
  • 又拟远别宸居 - Planning another long absence from the imperial residence.
  • 不返 - Not returning.
  • 陛下圣逾秦帝 - Your Majesty's virtue surpasses that of the Qin emperor.
  • 明甚隋皇 - Your Majesty's wisdom exceeds that of the Sui emperor.
  • 且无北筑之虞 - Without the worry of building northward.
  • 焉有南游之 - How can there be the trouble of southern tours.
  • 寛仁大度 - Broad-minded and benevolent.
  • 笃孝深慈 - Deeply filial and compassionate.
  • 知稼穑之艰难 - Understanding the hardships of farming.
  • 识古今之成败 - Knowing the successes and failures of past and present.
  • 自防得失 - Protecting oneself from gains and losses.
  • 不纵襟懐 - Not indulging in one's thoughts and feelings.
  • 忍教致却宗言道断 - Causing the advice of the wise to be ignored and the path of righteousness to be severed.
  • 使烝民以何托 - Leaving the common people without support.
  • 令慈母以何辜 - What sin has the kind mother committed.
  • 若何虑以危亡 - What is feared in terms of danger and destruction.
  • 但恐乖于仁孝 - Only fearing a deviation from benevolence and filial piety.

Cultural and Historical Allusions

  • 省方巡狩 - The practice of the emperor traveling to various regions to inspect governance and administer justice.
  • 吊民伐罪 - The mission of the emperor to console the people and punish the guilty.
  • 展义观风 - The emperor's role in extending righteousness and observing local customs.
  • 青城 - A place name, possibly referring to a specific location where an incident occurred.
  • 先皇 - The previous emperor, often used to refer to a respected predecessor.
  • 宫阙 - The palace, a symbol of imperial power and residence.
  • 鸾驾 - The imperial carriage, a symbol of the emperor's presence and authority.
  • 北筑 - Referring to the construction efforts in the north, possibly alluding to Qin Shi Huang's Great Wall.
  • 南游 - Southern tours, which were often seen as less necessary or more risky.
  • 稼穑 - Farming, a fundamental aspect of Chinese society.
  • 古今之成败 - The successes and failures of past and present, reflecting on historical lessons.
  • 自防得失 - Protecting oneself from both gains and losses, a balanced approach to governance.
  • 忍教致却宗言道断 - Causing the wise advice to be ignored and the path of righteousness to be severed, a critique of neglecting good counsel.
  • 烝民 - The common people, a term often used in classical Chinese literature.
  • 慈母 - A kind mother, used metaphorically to refer to the people or the state.
  • 仁孝 - Benevolence and filial piety, core values in Confucian ethics.

Proper Nouns

  • 先见 - Term of address for the emperor.
  • 青城 - A place name, possibly referring to a specific location.
  • 先皇 - The previous emperor.
  • 宫阙 - The palace.
  • 鸾驾 - The imperial carriage.
  • 北筑 - Construction efforts in the north.
  • 南游 - Southern tours.
  • 稼穑 - Farming.
  • 古今之成败 - The successes and failures of past and present.
  • 自防得失 - Protecting oneself from gains and losses.
  • 忍教致却宗言道断 - Causing the wise advice to be ignored and the path of righteousness to be severed.
  • 烝民 - The common people.
  • 慈母 - A kind mother, used metaphorically.
  • 仁孝 - Benevolence and filial piety.

[2834]

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 玉京金阙宝殿珠楼内苑上林琼池环圃 - Compound phrase describing a grand and luxurious imperial palace complex. '玉京' and '金阙' are poetic terms for the heavenly palace and the golden gate, respectively. '宝殿' and '珠楼' refer to valuable halls and pearl-like towers. '内苑' and '上林' are imperial gardens. '琼池' and '环圃' are jade pools and encircling gardens, emphasizing the opulence and beauty of the setting.
  • 香风满槛瑞露盈盘 - '香风满槛' means 'fragrant breeze fills the railing,' and '瑞露盈盘' means 'auspicious dew fills the tray.' These phrases describe a serene and blessed environment.
  • 钧天之乐奏九韶 - '钧天之乐' refers to the music of heaven, and '九韶' is an ancient and highly esteemed musical composition. This phrase suggests the highest form of celestial music.
  • 雪之舞呈八佾 - '雪之舞' means 'dance of snow,' and '八佾' refers to the eight rows of dancers in the Zhou Dynasty court ritual. This phrase describes a graceful and formal dance performance.
  • 簇神仙于清虚之境 - '簇神仙' means 'gathering of immortals,' and '清虚之境' means 'pure and empty realm.' This phrase describes a scene where immortals are gathered in a pure and ethereal place.
  • 列歌舞于阆苑之中 - '列歌舞' means 'arranging songs and dances,' and '阆苑' means 'Penglai Garden,' a mythical paradise. This phrase describes a scene where songs and dances are arranged in a mythical garden.
  • 人间胜致天下所无 - '人间胜致' means 'the finest scenes in the world,' and '天下所无' means 'without equal in the world.' This phrase emphasizes the unparalleled beauty of the described scenes.
  • 何必须于远塞看彼荒山 - '何必须于远塞' means 'why must one travel to distant frontiers,' and '看彼荒山' means 'to see those barren mountains.' This phrase questions the necessity of traveling to remote and desolate places.
  • 不惜圣躯有何裨益 - '不惜圣躯' means 'not sparing the body of a sage,' and '有何裨益' means 'what benefit is there?' This phrase criticizes the idea of risking one's health for no real gain.
  • 方今岐阳不顺梁园已亡 - '方今' means 'at present,' '岐阳' refers to a region, '不顺' means 'not obedient,' '梁园' refers to a palace, and '已亡' means 'has already fallen.' This phrase describes the current state of political unrest and loss.
  • 中原有人大事未了 - '中原有人' means 'there are people in the central plains,' and '大事未了' means 'major affairs are not yet completed.' This phrase highlights the ongoing challenges and unfinished business.
  • 且当国生灵受盗贼横行 - '且当国' means 'and in the country,' '生灵受' means 'the people are suffering,' and '盗贼横行' means 'bandits are rampant.' This phrase describes the suffering of the people and the prevalence of banditry.
  • 纵边廷无烽火之危而内地有腹心之患 - '纵边廷' means 'even if the border regions,' '无烽火之危' means 'are free from the danger of war,' '而内地' means 'but the interior,' and '有腹心之患' means 'has internal threats.' This phrase contrasts the relative peace at the borders with the internal turmoil.
  • 陛下千年膺运一国称尊 - '陛下' means 'Your Majesty,' '千年膺运' means 'thousands of years of good fortune,' and '一国称尊' means 'one nation under your rule.' This phrase praises the emperor's long and prosperous reign.
  • 文德武功经天纬地 - '文德武功' means 'moral virtue and martial prowess,' and '经天纬地' means 'to govern the heavens and earth.' This phrase describes the emperor's comprehensive and effective governance.
  • 孝逾于舜仁甚于汤 - '孝逾于舜' means 'more filial than Shun,' and '仁甚于汤' means 'more benevolent than Tang.' This phrase compares the emperor's virtues to those of legendary rulers.
  • 百行皆全万机不扰 - '百行皆全' means 'all virtues are complete,' and '万机不扰' means 'all affairs are well-managed.' This phrase emphasizes the emperor's perfect character and efficient administration.
  • 深智谋独懐英杰 - '深智谋' means 'deep strategic thinking,' and '独懐英杰' means 'uniquely harboring heroes.' This phrase highlights the emperor's strategic acumen and the presence of talented individuals.
  • 方居大寳正是少年 - '方居大寳' means 'currently occupying the great treasure' (the throne), and '正是少年' means 'is still young.' This phrase describes the emperor's youth and his position as ruler.
  • 既承社稷之基复把山河之险 - '既承社稷之基' means 'having inherited the foundation of the state,' and '复把山河之险' means 'and holding the perilous mountains and rivers.' This phrase emphasizes the emperor's responsibility and the challenges he faces.
  • 但不远听深察安居虑危 - '但不远听' means 'but not listening far away,' '深察' means 'deeply investigating,' '安居' means 'living in peace,' and '虑危' means 'considering dangers.' This phrase advises the emperor to listen closely, investigate deeply, and remain vigilant.
  • 辟四门以求贤 - '辟四门' means 'opening the four gates,' and '以求贤' means 'to seek the virtuous.' This phrase describes the practice of seeking talented individuals.
  • 总万而行事 - '总万' means 'summoning all the people,' and '而行事' means 'to act.' This phrase describes the emperor's role in governing the people.
  • 咸有一德端坐九重 - '咸有一德' means 'all have one virtue,' and '端坐九重' means 'sitting upright in the highest seat.' This phrase emphasizes the unity and righteousness of the emperor's actions.
  • 使恩威并行赏 - '使恩威并行' means 'to let grace and authority coexist,' and '赏' means 'reward.' This phrase describes the balanced approach to governance, combining rewards and punishments.

  • 緩辔逾双劒 - 緩辔 means to slow down or move slowly, and 逾双劒 refers to crossing the Double Swords (a metaphor for a challenging journey or path). Together, it suggests a cautious or deliberate passage through a difficult terrain.
  • 作千寻壁垒 - 作 means to create or build, 千寻 is a hyperbolic term for something extremely tall or grand, and 壁垒 refers to fortifications or defenses. This phrase likely means to construct grand or impenetrable defenses.
  • 道德虽无取 - 道德 refers to moral principles, 虽 means although, and 无取 means of no value or benefit. This phrase suggests that moral principles are of no practical benefit.
  • 江山粗可矜 - 江山 refers to the landscape or territory, 粗 means roughly or somewhat, and 矜 means to boast or take pride in. This phrase suggests that the landscape can be somewhat boasted of or taken pride in.
  • 孰敢振威棱 - 孰敢 means who dares, and 振威棱 means to display or maintain one's authority or power. This phrase suggests that no one dares to challenge or undermine one's authority.
  • 险固疑天设 - 险固 means dangerous and solid, and 疑天设 means to seem as if set by heaven. This phrase suggests that the terrain's danger and solidity seem as if divinely arranged.
  • 鸟道微通处 - 鸟道 refers to a narrow or difficult path, and 微通处 means a barely accessible place. This phrase suggests a barely accessible narrow path.
  • 李杜常挨托 - 李杜 refers to Li Bai and Du Fu, two famous poets, and 常挨托 means often rely on or depend on. This phrase suggests that Li Bai and Du Fu often relied on something.
  • 庸才安可守 - 庸才 means mediocre talent, and 安可守 means how can it be maintained. This phrase suggests that mediocre talent cannot be maintained.
  • 上德始堪矜 - 上德 means high virtue, and 始堪矜 means only worthy of boasting. This phrase suggests that only high virtue is worthy of boasting.
  • 暗指长天路 - 暗指 means to subtly point to, 长天 refers to the vast sky, and 路 means path. This phrase suggests subtly pointing to a path in the vast sky.
  • 浓峦蔽几层 - 浓峦 refers to dense mountains, and 蔽几层 means to cover several layers. This phrase suggests that dense mountains cover several layers, creating a sense of depth and mystery.
  • 先朝神武力开边 - 先朝 means the previous dynasty, 神武 means divine and martial, and 力开边 means to forcefully expand the borders. This phrase suggests that the previous dynasty forcefully expanded the borders through divine and martial power.
  • 画断封疆四 - 画断 means to draw or divide, 封疆 refers to borders, and 四 means four. This phrase suggests dividing the borders into four parts.

  • 五千前望陇山屯劒防后慿巫峡鏁峰烟 - A poetic description combining geographical features and military imagery, describing a strategic defensive position.
  • 轩皇尚自亲平宼 - Refers to the Yellow Emperor (Xuanyuan Huangdi) personally leading the fight against bandits, a legendary event.
  • 嬴政徒劳爱学仙 - Refers to Qin Shi Huang (Ying Zheng) vainly seeking immortality through alchemy and other practices.
  • 想到隗宫寻胜处 - Implies a search for a place of victory or triumph, possibly referring to a historical site.
  • 正应鸎语暮春天 - Describes the scene of spring evening, with the sound of orioles, evoking a peaceful and serene atmosphere.
  • 龙斾飘飖指极边 - Describes the dragon banner (a symbol of imperial authority) fluttering towards the distant frontier.
  • 到时犹更二三千 - Implies that the journey or campaign will extend over a distance of several thousand miles.
  • 冐冷朝冲断续烟 - Describes the cold morning air and the intermittent smoke, possibly from fires or fog.
  • 自学汉皇开土宇 - Refers to the Han Emperor (likely referring to Liu Bang, the founder of the Han Dynasty) expanding the empire.
  • 不同周穆好神仙 - Contrasts the Han Emperor with King Mu of Zhou, who was known for his interest in immortality.
  • 秦民莫遣无恩及 - Implies that the people of Qin should not be neglected or left without favors.
  • 大散关东别有天 - Describes a significant geographical or political boundary, possibly referring to the Great Scattered Pass.
  • 洎至利州已闻东师下固鎭矣 - Indicates that by the time they reached Lishui (a city in Sichuan), they had heard that the eastern army had already secured the area.
  • 旬日内又闻金牛败卒塞峡而至 - Implies that within a few days, they heard that defeated soldiers from Jinshui had blocked the gorge.
  • 其时蜀师十余万自绵汉至于深渡千余里首尾相继皆无心鬭敌 - Describes the large number of Shu forces stretching over a thousand miles, with no intention of fighting.
  • 遣使臣逼促则枪刺之曰请换取龙武军相战不惟勇敢况且偏请衣粮我等拣退不堪何能相杀实无奈何 - Describes the Shu forces' refusal to fight, even when threatened, and their plea for better-equipped troops.
  • 十月二十九日狼狈而归于栈阁悬险溪岩壑之中连夜继昼却入成都 - Describes the chaotic retreat back to Chengdu, navigating through dangerous mountain passes and ravines.
  • 康延孝与魏王继踵而入少主 - Describes the entry of Kang Yanxiao and the young prince (likely referring to the Later Shu dynasty).

Idiomatic Expressions

  • 于是 - Temporal conjunction meaning 'at that time' or 'thereupon,' used to introduce a sequence of events.
  • 未入前 - Temporal phrase meaning 'before entering' or 'prior to the entry.'
  • 兵刃不举 - Phrase meaning 'weapons were not raised,' suggesting a lack of conflict.
  • 既知东军入蜀 - Phrase meaning 'after knowing that the Eastern army had entered Shu,' indicating awareness of an event.
  • 遂拥麾下之师及妇女孩幼万余口金银缯帛于西蕃买路归蜀 - Complex phrase meaning 'thereupon, he gathered his troops and tens of thousands of women, children, and servants, along with gold, silver, silk, and other valuables, to buy a passage back to Shu through the Western Turks.'
  • 防路为左袵掳夺 - Phrase meaning 'along the way, they were plundered by the left-side robe people,' referring to the Turks.
  • 并经溪山冻饿相践而死 - Phrase meaning 'they perished by freezing, starving, and trampling each other in the mountains and streams.'
  • 迨至蜀存者百余人 - Phrase meaning 'when they finally reached Shu, only a hundred or so survived.'
  • 唯与田宗汭等脱身而至 - Phrase meaning 'only with Tian Zongrui and others did they manage to escape.'
  • 亲握鋭兵何得不战 - Phrase meaning 'Why didn't you fight when you personally held sharp troops?'
  • 何不早降 - Phrase meaning 'Why didn't you surrender earlier?'
  • 其初入蕃部几许人同行 - Phrase meaning 'How many people were there when you first entered the Turkic territory?'
  • 才及百数 - Phrase meaning 'Only about a hundred.'
  • 汝可偿此万人之命 - Phrase meaning 'You can atone for the lives of these ten thousand people.'
  • 遂尽斩之 - Phrase meaning 'So they were all executed.'
  • 蜀师不战坐取亡灭者 - Phrase meaning 'The Shu army, without fighting, passively faced its downfall.'
  • 人多不知之 - Phrase meaning 'Many people did not know this.'

  • 同乃指段恪若不日暗何得短人行坐中大笑 - '同乃指段恪' means 'Xuan Tong then pointed to Duan Ke,' '若不日暗何得短人行坐中大笑' translates to 'if not for the darkness, how could he be so short that he can't walk or sit properly in the room, causing everyone to laugh.' This phrase describes a humorous scene where Xuan Tong points out someone's short stature in the dark, leading to laughter.
  • 仓曹曰何不先将粪粥来举坐咸笑之 - '仓曹曰' means 'the warehouse officer said,' '何不先将粪粥来' translates to 'why not bring the fecal porridge first,' and '举坐咸笑之' means 'everyone in the room laughed at him.' This phrase describes a situation where the warehouse officer's comment about porridge causes laughter.
  • 仓曹乃是公侯之子孙必复其始诸君何为笑也 - '仓曹乃是公侯之子孙' means 'the warehouse officer is a descendant of a noble family,' '必复其始' translates to 'must return to his roots,' and '诸君何为笑也' means 'why are you laughing at him?' This phrase suggests that the warehouse officer's noble background should be respected, and the laughter is inappropriate.
  • 清斋同在幕内坐有犬来遗粪于墙上同乃取支牀塼自击之傍人怪其率问曰何为自彻支牀塼打狗同曰可不闻茍利社稷专之亦可 - '清斋同在幕内坐' means 'Xuan Tong was sitting in the tent during a clean fast,' '有犬来遗粪于墙上' translates to 'a dog came and left feces on the wall,' '同乃取支牀塼自击之' means 'Xuan Tong then took a support brick and hit it himself,' '傍人怪其率问曰何为自彻支牀塼打狗' translates to 'the bystanders were surprised by his directness and asked why he was breaking a support brick to hit the dog,' and '同曰可不闻茍利社稷专之亦可' means 'Xuan Tong replied, 'Have you not heard that if it is for the benefit of the state, one can act decisively?'' This passage describes a humorous and somewhat dramatic scene where Xuan Tong reacts to a dog's behavior in a way that surprises others but is justified by his commitment to the state.

'唐先天中' means 'during the early Tang Dynasty', '姜师度于长安城中穿渠绕朝堂坊市无所不至' means 'Jiang Shidu dug channels around the imperial palace, markets, and streets in Chang'an', '上登西楼望之' means 'he climbed up to the west tower to look at it', '师度堰水泷柴栰而下' means 'Jiang Shidu built a dam to control the water', '遂授司农卿' means 'he was appointed as the Minister of Agriculture', '于后水涨则奔突水缩则竭涸' means 'later, when the water rose, it rushed, and when it receded, it dried up', '又前开黄河引水向棣州' means 'he also opened the Yellow River to divert water to Di Prefecture', '费亿兆功百姓苦其淹渍' means 'it cost millions of laborers, and the people suffered from flooding', '又役夫塞河口' means 'he also used laborers to block the river mouth', '开元六年水泛溢河口堰破棣州百姓一槩没尽' means 'in the sixth year of Kaiyuan, the water overflowed, the dam broke, and the people in Di Prefecture were all drowned', '师度以为功官品益进' means 'Jiang Shidu considered it a success and his official rank increased', '又有傅孝忠为太史令自言明象专行矫谲京中语曰姜师度一心看地孝忠两眼相天神武即位知其矫并斩之' means 'there was also Fu Xiaozhong, the Chief Astronomer, who claimed to be skilled in astronomy and specialized in deceit, in the capital, people said, 'Jiang Shidu focuses on the ground, while Fu Xiaozhong looks at the sky; when Emperor Shengwu ascended the throne, he knew they were deceitful and had them executed'.

  • 北梦琐言 - Historical record compiled by Li Fang, providing insights into the Tang Dynasty and its officials.
